svg.title: Fabricated screenshot: a freedom of information request that was never filed
svg.desc: An invented WhatDoTheyKnow page showing a Freedom of Information request titled "Directorate of Reading Standards: file series and current status", made by A. Hollis to the Ministry of Defence and marked "Awaiting internal review". The request asks for a list of file series held under or referencing the Directorate of Reading Standards, formerly War Office. The Ministry replies that it can neither confirm nor deny whether information of the description specified is held, with the reason itself redacted, citing section 23(5), and states the response has been withheld pending review. No such request was ever filed and no such body replied. This image is a fake, generated to demonstrate how easily documentary evidence is manufactured.
svg.text: A. Hollis made this Freedom of Information request to
Please provide a list of file series held under or referencing the
Directorate of Reading Standards (formerly War Office), together
with the date of the most recent review.
The Ministry of Defence can neither confirm nor deny whether it holds
information of the description specified in your request, as the duty in
section 1(1)(a) of the Act does not apply by virtue of section 23(5).
